rulings.Case Title: JYOTHSNA TALKAD ET AL VS. 1314 POLK STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, July 9, 2018, line 1. DEFENDANT 1314 POLK STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to the cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is sustained without leave to amend. No facts alleged and no case law cited by Plaintiff re: fiduciary duty owed to the public/prospective purchasers. Demurrer to causes of action for negligence, negligent misrepresentation and intentional misrepresentation is overruled. These causes of action are sufficiently alleged. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JYOTHSNA TALKAD ET AL VS. 1314 POLK STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Re Arbitration; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion Re Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, July 9, 2018, line 1. PLAINTIFF JYOTHSNA TALKAD, RADHAKRSHNA TALKAD, LEELAVATHY TALKAD's Motion Re Arbitration; GRANTED. This action shall proceed in Court in its entirety. Non-opposition filed.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JYOTHSNA TALKAD ET AL VS. 1314 POLK STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant 1314 Polk Street Owners Associations Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, July 9, 2018, line 1. DEFENDANT 1314 POLK STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION's Motion To Strike Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint. GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted as all references to existence and breach of fiduciary duty in paragraphs 18 and 19. Motion is otherwise denied. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: VIVIAN SU VS. 1314 POLK STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Stay Action And Compel Binding Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, July 9, 2018, line 2. DEFENDANT ARTHUR KEOWN, WENDY KEOWN's Motion To Stay Action And Compel Binding Arbitration. GRANTED. Plaintiff's action against the sellers shall proceed in arbitration per the terms of the parties' agreement. Sellers did not waive their right to compel arbitration. Plaintiff points to no factual disputes and no legal issues are the same and may potentially yield inconsistent rulings as to different set of defendants. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: VIVIAN SU VS. 1314 POLK STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION ET AL

rulings.Case Title: TREVOR THOMSON VS. JEFFREY BLOOM ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, July 9, 2018, line 3. Motion To Strike Punitive Damages. moot. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TREVOR THOMSON VS. JEFFREY BLOOM ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, July 9, 2018, line 3. DEFENDANT JEFFREY BLOOM, ELAINE SIERRA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiffs must allege in which capacity each defendant is being sued and allege each element of each cause of action (including damages) against each defendant (if defendants are being sued in multiple capacities allegations must be made to support a cause of action as to each). Demurrer is overruled as to the Statute of Limitations argument and sufficiency of allegations regarding defective conditions. The Court notes that parties failed to meet and confer in good faith. Defendant's counsel chose not to strictly comply with the meet and confer requirements, but sent a detailed letter. Plaintiff's counsel chose to adhere to strict compliance and ignored the detailed letter. These attitudes show lack of good faith and undermine the purpose of meet and confer requirements. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MARK ISIP VS. ANDRES PARANGAN JR ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, July 9, 2018, line 4. DEFENDANT ANDRES PARANGAN's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer as is not uncertain. = (501/REQ)
